name: Complete Blood Count (CBC) / Hemoglobin
measures: The number and quality of red blood cells, white blood cells, and platelets, including your hemoglobin level.
whyRecommended: Helps detect anaemia, which is common in pregnancy, so iron and overall blood health can be supported early.

name: Blood Group & Rh Typing
measures: Your blood group (A, B, AB, or O) and whether you are Rh positive or Rh negative.
whyRecommended: Identifies Rh-negative pregnancies that may need special care, and provides important information in case a transfusion is ever needed.

name: Blood Sugar / Oral Glucose Tolerance Test (OGTT)
measures: How your body processes sugar by checking blood glucose levels, often before and after a measured glucose drink.
whyRecommended: Screens for gestational diabetes so that blood sugar can be managed to support a healthy pregnancy for you and your baby.

name: Thyroid Function (TSH)
measures: The level of thyroid-stimulating hormone, which reflects how well the thyroid gland is working.
whyRecommended: Thyroid hormones are important for the baby's brain development, so checking helps ensure levels stay in a healthy range.

name: Ferritin / Iron Studies
measures: Your body's iron stores, including ferritin, which shows how much iron is available in reserve.
whyRecommended: Gives a fuller picture of iron status than hemoglobin alone, helping guide diet and supplementation to prevent anaemia.

name: Vitamin D
measures: The level of vitamin D in your blood.
whyRecommended: Vitamin D supports calcium absorption and bone health for both you and your baby, and deficiency is common.

name: Infection Screen (HIV, Hepatitis B, Syphilis, Rubella)
measures: Markers of certain infections and immunity, including HIV, hepatitis B, syphilis, and rubella immunity.
whyRecommended: Early detection allows steps that protect the baby's health, and rubella status guides advice about immunity and future vaccination.

name: Urine Routine & Culture
measures: Protein, sugar, and signs of infection in the urine, with a culture to identify any bacteria.
whyRecommended: Detects urinary infections and early signs of conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes so they can be addressed promptly.
